tempos.tempo_description: Etwas lebhaft und mit der innigsten Empfindung. Allegretto, ma non troppo
Langsam und sehnsuchtvoll. Adagio, ma non troppo, con affetto
Zeitmaß des ersten Stückes. Tempo del primo pezzo: tutto il Cembalo, ma piano
Geschwinde, doch nicht zu sehr, und mit Entschlossenheit. Allegro

subpieces.first_line: Jetzt, Schätzchen, jetzt sind wir allein
O wär ich schon mit dir vereint
Hat man nicht auch Gold beineben
Jetzt, Alter, jetzt hat es Eile!
Wie kalt ist es in diesem unterirdischen Gewölbe!
Euch werde Lohn in bessern Welten
Er sterbe! Doch er soll erst wissen

roles.name: Leonore
roles.description: Florestan's wife, disguised as a man under the alias Fidelio

title: Six Variations on an Original Theme

tempos.tempo_description: Allegretto ma non troppo quasi Andante con moto
Entr'acte IV. Poco sostenuto e risoluto
Adagio ma non tanto e cantabile
Mit Lebhaftigkeit und durchaus mit Empfindung und Ausdruck
Nicht zu geschwind und sehr singbar vorgetragen
Andante cantabile, ma però con moto
Auf dem Hügel sitz ich spähend
Es kehret der Maien, es blühet die Au
Nimm sie hin denn Diese Lieder

title: Musik zu einem Ritterballett (Music for a ballet on horseback) (1790–01)
Six minuets for orchestra (original version lost, only an arr. for piano is extant) (1795)
Musical joke for three voices 'Lob auf den Dicken'
Musical joke for three voices and chorus 'Graf, Graf, liebster Graf'
'Gesang der Mönche' from Schiller's Wilhelm Tell for three male voices
Seven Ländler for two violins and cello (original version lost, only an arr. for piano is extant) (1799)
Elegie auf den Tod eines Pudels
Twelve German Dances for orchestra (original version lost, only an arr. for piano is extant) (1792–97)
Als die Geliebte sich trennen wollte
Twelve contredanses for orchestra (nos. 1–2, 4–5, 7–10, 12, later arranged for piano, Hess 102) (1791–1801)
Six Ländler for two violins and double bass (later arranged for piano) (1802)
Der edle Mensch sei hilfreich und gut
Eleven Mödlinger Tänze for seven instruments (probably spurious) (1819)
March for Military Band 'Für die Böhmische Landwehr' ['For the Bohemian Ward'] (later arranged for piano, Hess 99) (1809)
Musical joke 'Ich bin der Herr von zu'
Riddle canon 'Das Schöne zu dem Guten' (first version)
Riddle canon 'Das Schöne zu dem Guten' (second version)
Ten musical quips (Kinsky's word is 'Notenscherze') from Beethoven's letters
Oboe Concerto in F major (lost; only incipits and draft of 2nd movement extant) (Hess 12)
Romance cantabile for soloists and orchestra (Hess 13)
Minuet in A♭ for string quartet (Hess 33, piano version Hess 88)
Allegretto for string quartet in B minor (Pencarrow Quartet, Gardi 16)
Anglaise for piano in D major (Hess 61)
Allegro (bagatelle) in A major (second part of the Allegro in A♭ and A, Biamonti 284)
Bagatelle in C major for piano (Hess 73)
Waltz or Ländler in C minor (Hess 68)
Écossaise for Military Band (only a piano arr. by Carl Czerny is extant) (1810)
Rondo for wind octet (believed to be the original finale of the Octet, opus 103) (1792)
Three duets for clarinet and bassoon (probably spurious)
Variations for two oboes and English horn on 'Là ci darem la mano' from Mozart's opera Don Giovanni (1795)
March for Wind Sextet in B♭ (1797–98)
Triumphal March for orchestra for Christoph Kuffner [de]'s tragedy Tarpeja (1813)
Introduction to Act II of Leonore (1805)
Three Equale for four trombones (vocal arrs. of these were performed at Beethoven's funeral) (1812)
Duo for viola and cello, 'mit zwei obligaten Augengläsern' ('with two obbligato eyeglasses') (1796–97)
Trio for Piano, Flute, and Bassoon
Allegretto for piano trio in B♭ major (1812)
Piano Concerto No. 0 in E♭ major (solo part only with indications of orchestration) (1784)

Rondo for piano and violin in G major (1793–94)
Six German Dances for violin and piano (1796)
Andante and variations for mandolin and piano (1796)

Seven Variations on "Bei Männern, welche Liebe fühlen"
Rondo for piano in C major (1783)
Rondo for piano in A major (1783)
Violin Concerto movement in C major, fragment (1790–92)
Piano Sonatina (?) in F major (1790–92, two subpieces)
Presto (Bagatelle) for piano in C minor (1795)
Lustig-Traurig (Bagatelle) for piano in C major (1802)
Prelude for piano in F minor (1803)
Allegretto (Bagatelle) for piano in C major (1803)
Cadenzas for 1st and 3rd subpieces of Mozart's D minor Piano Concerto (K. 466) (1809)
Rondo in B♭ major for piano and orchestra, possibly part of initial version of the Piano Concerto No. 2 (1793)
Allegretto for piano in B minor (1821)
Allegretto quasi andante for piano in G minor (1825)
String Quintet in C major [scores] (fragment, piano trans.)
Nine variations for piano on a march by Ernst Christoph Dressler (1782)
Six Variations on a Swiss song for piano or harp (1790–1792)
Twenty-four variations for piano on Vincenzo Righini's aria 'Venni Amore' (1790–1791)
Thirteen variations for piano on the aria 'Es war einmal ein alter Mann' from Carl Ditters von Dittersdorf's opera Das rote Käppchen (1792)
Eight variations for piano four hands on a theme by Count Waldstein (1792)
Twelve variations for piano on the 'Menuet a la Vigano' from Jakob Haibel's ballet Le nozze disturbate (1795)
Nine variations for piano on 'Quant'e piu bello' from Giovanni Paisiello's opera La Molinara (1795)
Twelve minuets for orchestra (later arranged for piano, Hess 101) (1795)
Six Variations on "Nel cor più non mi sento"

title: Fünf Lieder nach Gedichten von Stefan George

tempos.tempo_description: Ja Heil und Dank. Sehr langsam
So ich traurig bin. Sehr fließend und zart
Ihr tratet zu dem Herde. Langsam
Du, der ich nicht sage. Langsam
Du machst mich allein. Sehr langsam
